bead.title: agent-mode tests: prove workers ignore stewardship defaults
bead.description:
PROBLEM
The guidance change is risky in two directions: it must stop broad interactive sessions from opportunistically editing ready beads, and it must not suppress non-interactive ddx work execution. The project needs deterministic regression coverage for both sides.

ROOT CAUSE
cli/internal/agent/execute_bead_guardrails_test.go contains load-bearing prompt invariant tests, but it does not assert a DDX mode boundary. Existing work-loop tests exercise no-ready-work and execution behavior, but they do not prove the new interactive queue-steward guidance is read-only or that ddx work --once still executes a ready bead after the guidance lands. The combined interactive-steward skill bead owns the fixture-backed route table and reference guidance that separate broad interactive prompts from worker prompts.

PROPOSED FIX
Add a focused regression layer that tests representative prompt routes and the worker carve-out. Use deterministic fixtures/static assertions rather than requiring an LLM call: broad interactive prompts should produce queue-steward guidance with no code-edit instruction, while ddx work/try worker prompts should produce bead_execution guidance and remain implementation-capable.

NON-SCOPE
- Benchmarking model quality.
- Running external harnesses or networked agents in unit tests.
- Replacing existing execute-bead integration tests.

PARENT AND DEPS
Parent: ddx-09d7d787. Dependencies: ddx-f65f1394, ddx-3c6ca63f, ddx-c2a66f45-wt-6bee7238, and ddx-c2a66f45-wt-53f8c8b8.
bead.acceptance:
1. Add a deterministic routing fixture or table covering at least "what's blocking the queue now", "what should I work on next", "work the queue", "ddx work --once", and an explicit direct implementation request.
2. TestDDxQueueStewardGuidance_InteractiveBlockingQuestionIsReadOnly asserts broad blocking/queue prompts route to queue_steward, prefer focus/status inspection, and do not instruct the agent to edit code or execute a ready bead manually.
3. TestDDxModeRoutingPrompts_DoWorkPrefersWorkerCommand asserts ambiguous "work the queue" guidance recommends ddx work/worker dispatch instead of local manual execution.
4. TestDDxWorkOnce_BeadExecutionIgnoresQueueStewardDefault asserts ddx work --once prompt/runtime path carries bead_execution and still permits implementing bead AC.
5. TestDDxDirectImplementationMode_AllowsExplicitUserEdits asserts explicit human requests to modify code/docs are not blocked by queue-steward guidance.
6. cd cli && go test ./cmd/... ./internal/agent/... -run "TestDDxQueueStewardGuidance_InteractiveBlockingQuestionIsReadOnly|TestDDxModeRoutingPrompts_DoWorkPrefersWorkerCommand|TestDDxWorkOnce_BeadExecutionIgnoresQueueStewardDefault|TestDDxDirectImplementationMode_AllowsExplicitUserEdits" -count=1 passes.
7. cd cli && go test ./cmd/... ./internal/agent/... passes.
8. lefthook run pre-commit passes.
